item.title: Dynamical signatures and control of time-reversal symmetry breaking in twisted nodal superconductors
item.description:
Author(s): Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov

Recent observations of time-reversal breaking superconductivity at twisted cuprate interfaces motivate the development of new approaches to better characterize this emergent phenomenon. Here we study the dynamical properties of the order parameters at the twisted unconventional superconductor interf…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014513] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Dynamical signatures and control of time-reversal symmetry breaking in twisted nodal superconductors
item.dc:creator: Jefferson Tang and Pavel A. Volkov
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Superfluidity and superconductivity
item.prism:section: Superfluidity and superconductivity

item.title: Symmetry-based real-space framework for realizing flat bands and discovering nodal-line touchings
item.description:
Author(s): Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu

The authors introduce here a systematic and unified real-space recipe to build flat bands from symmetric compact localized states. Multiple examples across different lattice symmetries, orbital contents and dimensions validate the versatility of the scheme. In addition, the authors derive concise criteria for determining all topological band crossings in flat-band systems — including intriguing nodal type — and demonstrate their intimate connection to underlying symmetries. The findings provide a generic theoretical framework for studying flat band models and materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035140] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu

The authors introduce here a systematic and unified real-space recipe to build flat bands from symmetric compact localized states. Multiple examples across different lattice symmetries, orbital contents and dimensions validate the versatility of the scheme. In addition, the authors derive concise criteria for determining all topological band crossings in flat-band systems — including intriguing nodal type — and demonstrate their intimate connection to underlying symmetries. The findings provide a generic theoretical framework for studying flat band models and materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035140] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Symmetry-based real-space framework for realizing flat bands and discovering nodal-line touchings
item.dc:creator: Rui-Heng Liu and Xin Liu
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes
item.description:
Author(s): Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel

Braiding and fusion of Majorana zero modes are key elements of any future topological Majorana-based quantum computer. Here, we investigate the fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes in the spinless Kitaev model, as well as in a spinful model describing magnet-superconductor hybrid structures. We co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035429] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel

Braiding and fusion of Majorana zero modes are key elements of any future topological Majorana-based quantum computer. Here, we investigate the fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes in the spinless Kitaev model, as well as in a spinful model describing magnet-superconductor hybrid structures. We co…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035429] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Fusion dynamics of Majorana zero modes
item.dc:creator: Themba Hodge, Tuan Kieu, Jasmin Bedow, Eric Mascot, Dirk K. Morr, and Stephan Rachel
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Two-site Kitaev sweet spots evolving into topological islands
item.description:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado

Here, the authors demonstrate that Majorana bound states can be identified through a simple electrical transport measurement in artificial Kitaev chains composed of quantum dots coupled by superconductors. The key result is that the non-Abelian Majorana algebra – specifically the property γ2=½ – maps directly onto conductance. When this condition is met, the conductance locks to a robust half-quantized plateau e2/2h, signaling the emergence of “topological islands” with protected Majorana modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035432] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado

Here, the authors demonstrate that Majorana bound states can be identified through a simple electrical transport measurement in artificial Kitaev chains composed of quantum dots coupled by superconductors. The key result is that the non-Abelian Majorana algebra – specifically the property γ2=½ – maps directly onto conductance. When this condition is met, the conductance locks to a robust half-quantized plateau e2/2h, signaling the emergence of “topological islands” with protected Majorana modes.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035432] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Two-site Kitaev sweet spots evolving into topological islands
item.dc:creator: Rodrigo A. Dourado, J. Carlos Egues, and Poliana H. Penteado
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited

item.title: Surface topological quantum criticality II: Conformal manifolds, isolated fixed points, and entanglement
item.description:
Author(s): Saran Vijayan and Fei Zhou

In this article, we propose a possibility of realizing a distinct family of scale-conformal invariant interacting Hamiltonians in two-dimensional quantum many-body systems. Such phenomena can occur in various interacting systems, including topological surfaces or two-dimensional bulks. Based on a pr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045134] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Saran Vijayan and Fei Zhou

In this article, we propose a possibility of realizing a distinct family of scale-conformal invariant interacting Hamiltonians in two-dimensional quantum many-body systems. Such phenomena can occur in various interacting systems, including topological surfaces or two-dimensional bulks. Based on a pr…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045134] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.title: Connection between free-fermion and interacting crystalline symmetry-protected topological phases
item.description:
Author(s): Chen-Shen Lee, Ken Shiozaki, and Chang-Tse Hsieh

We present a framework for investigating the effects of interactions on crystalline symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phases. Within this framework, one can establish a direct connection between the equivalence classes of free-fermion systems and their corresponding interacting classes. A central…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045135] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Chen-Shen Lee, Ken Shiozaki, and Chang-Tse Hsieh

We present a framework for investigating the effects of interactions on crystalline symmetry-protected topological (SPT) phases. Within this framework, one can establish a direct connection between the equivalence classes of free-fermion systems and their corresponding interacting classes. A central…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045135] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.dc:subject: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems
item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Collapsing of spin-split Fermi surfaces in the magnetic Weyl semimetal candidate EuAuSb
item.description:
Author(s): Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

Strong coupling between magnetic moments and itinerant electrons can drive topological phase transitions and Fermi surface reconstructions, offering promising routes toward topological quantum computing. Here, the authors report a rare instance of Fermi surface splitting and collapse in the magnetic Weyl semimetal EuAuSb, revealed by temperature dependent Shubnikov–de Haas quantum oscillation measurements. These phenomena are attributed to the progressive weakening of short-range magnetic correlations and the exchange coupling between conduction electrons and localized magnetic moments with increasing temperature.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045138] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

Strong coupling between magnetic moments and itinerant electrons can drive topological phase transitions and Fermi surface reconstructions, offering promising routes toward topological quantum computing. Here, the authors report a rare instance of Fermi surface splitting and collapse in the magnetic Weyl semimetal EuAuSb, revealed by temperature dependent Shubnikov–de Haas quantum oscillation measurements. These phenomena are attributed to the progressive weakening of short-range magnetic correlations and the exchange coupling between conduction electrons and localized magnetic moments with increasing temperature.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045138] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026
item.dc:title: Collapsing of spin-split Fermi surfaces in the magnetic Weyl semimetal candidate EuAuSb
item.dc:creator: Juntao Yao, Zhixiang Hu, Niraj Aryal, Asish K. Kundu, Antu Laha, Chuhang Liu, Lijun Wu, Yimei Zhu, Elio Vescovo, Weiguo Yin, and Qiang Li

item.title: Nonequilibrium statistics of a biased Kondo resonance
item.description:
Author(s): Jong E. Han

Numerical renormalization group (NRG) is formulated for nonequilibrium steady state by converting finite-lattice many-body eigenstates into scattering states. Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

Extension of the full-density-matrix NRG for a biased Anderson impurity model, simplified by using the original orbital basis of the Hamilton…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045141] Published Wed Jan 21, 2026

item.description:

Recent studies have demonstrated that topological quantum spin Hall (QSH) states exist in twisted bilayers of transition metal dichalcogenides. However, a comprehensive theoretical characterization of the topological edge states remains a topic of interest and an unresolved issue. Here, the topologi…

item.title: Unconventional magnetism in spin-orbit coupled systems
item.description:
Author(s): Jian-Keng Yuan, Zhiming Pan, and Congjun Wu

“Unconventional magnetism” was proposed to describe the exotic states arising from Landau-Pomeranchuk instabilities in the spin channel nearly two decades ago. Its odd-partial-wave-channel (e.g., $p$-wave) states break parity giving rise to the dynamic generation of spin-orbit coupling, while its ev…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014426] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jian-Keng Yuan, Zhiming Pan, and Congjun Wu

“Unconventional magnetism” was proposed to describe the exotic states arising from Landau-Pomeranchuk instabilities in the spin channel nearly two decades ago. Its odd-partial-wave-channel (e.g., p-wave) states break parity giving rise to the dynamic generation of spin-orbit coupling, while its even…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014426] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Unconventional magnetism in spin-orbit coupled systems

item.title: Lithium impurity induced electron doping and in-gap states in high-${T}_{c}$ monolayer ${\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{Te},\mathrm{Se})/\mathrm{SrTiO}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Yuxuan Lei, Zhuoran Xu, Guanyang He, Yu Li, Yi Liu, Yanzhao Liu, and Jian Wang

Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) film grown on $\mathrm{SrTi}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ (STO) substrate has attracted considerable interest due to its interface-enhanced high-temperature (high-${T}_{c}$) superconductivity and potential topological electronic properties. While electron doping is widely considered a key fa…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014512] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Yuxuan Lei, Zhuoran Xu, Guanyang He, Yu Li, Yi Liu, Yanzhao Liu, and Jian Wang

Monolayer Fe(Te,Se) film grown on SrTiO3 (STO) substrate has attracted considerable interest due to its interface-enhanced high-temperature (high-Tc) superconductivity and potential topological electronic properties. While electron doping is widely considered a key factor in enhancing superconductiv…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 014512] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Lithium impurity induced electron doping and in-gap states in high-${T}_{c}$ monolayer ${\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{Te},\mathrm{Se})/\mathrm{SrTiO}}_{3}$

item.title: Disorder-driven exceptional points and concurrent topological phase transitions in non-Hermitian systems
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Cheng, Tiantao Qu, Yaqing Yang, Jun Chen, and Lei Zhang

Exceptional points (EPs) are spectral degeneracies unique to non-Hermitian systems which underpin phenomena from enhanced sensing to unconventional topology. While disorder is usually viewed as detrimental, it can also drive topological phase transitions (TPTs). Here, we show that random disorder al…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024203] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaoyu Cheng, Tiantao Qu, Yaqing Yang, Jun Chen, and Lei Zhang

Exceptional points (EPs) are spectral degeneracies unique to non-Hermitian systems which underpin phenomena from enhanced sensing to unconventional topology. While disorder is usually viewed as detrimental, it can also drive topological phase transitions (TPTs). Here, we show that random disorder al…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024203] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Disorder-driven exceptional points and concurrent topological phase transitions in non-Hermitian systems

item.title: Altermagnetism and its induced higher-order topology on the Lieb lattice
item.description:
Author(s): Xingmin Huo, Xingchuan Zhu, Chang-An Li, Shiping Feng, Song-Bo Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, and Huaiming Guo

Altermagnetism (AM) has brought renewed attention to the Lieb lattice. Here, we broaden the scope of altermagnetic models on the Lieb lattice by using a general scheme based on spin clusters. We design various altermagnetic models with $d$ and $g$ wave on the Lieb lattice and investigate its interpl…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024420] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xingmin Huo, Xingchuan Zhu, Chang-An Li, Shiping Feng, Song-Bo Zhang, Shengyuan A. Yang, and Huaiming Guo

Altermagnetism (AM) has brought renewed attention to the Lieb lattice. Here, we broaden the scope of altermagnetic models on the Lieb lattice by using a general scheme based on spin clusters. We design various altermagnetic models with d and g wave on the Lieb lattice and investigate its interplay w…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024420] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Altermagnetism and its induced higher-order topology on the Lieb lattice

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Single crystal growth, structural and physical properties, and absence of a charge density wave in ${\mathrm{Ti}}_{0.85}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{6}{\mathrm{Ge}}_{6}$
item.description:
Author(s): Dechao Cheng, Nour Maraytta, Xiuhua Chen, Xizhi Li, Xueliang Wu, Xiangxiang Jing, Yong Hu, Youpin Gong, Mingquan He, Yisheng Chai, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Pengfei Jiang, Yilin Wang, Michael Merz, and Aifeng Wang

The rattling chain model predicts CDWs in AM6X6 kagome compounds with small filler atoms A embedded in a large M6X6 host. Here, despite Ti being a small filler atom, the authors observe no CDW in Ti0.85Fe6Ge6. Through in-depth electronic structure and bonding analyses, they demonstrate that, in addition to ionic radius, the bonding characteristics of the filler atom are crucial for CDW formation. This finding significantly extends the rattling chain model, offering new insights into the factors driving CDW transitions in kagome materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035133] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dechao Cheng, Nour Maraytta, Xiuhua Chen, Xizhi Li, Xueliang Wu, Xiangxiang Jing, Yong Hu, Youpin Gong, Mingquan He, Yisheng Chai, Xiaoyuan Zhou, Pengfei Jiang, Yilin Wang, Michael Merz, and Aifeng Wang

The rattling chain model predicts CDWs in AM6X6 kagome compounds with small filler atoms A embedded in a large M6X6 host. Here, despite Ti being a small filler atom, the authors observe no CDW in Ti0.85Fe6Ge6. Through in-depth electronic structure and bonding analyses, they demonstrate that, in addition to ionic radius, the bonding characteristics of the filler atom are crucial for CDW formation. This finding significantly extends the rattling chain model, offering new insights into the factors driving CDW transitions in kagome materials.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035133] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Single crystal growth, structural and physical properties, and absence of a charge density wave in ${\mathrm{Ti}}_{0.85}{\mathrm{Fe}}_{6}{\mathrm{Ge}}_{6}$

item.title: Filling-dependent phase competition in the interacting kagome flat band system
item.description:
Author(s): Kevin J. U. Vidarte, R. Cardias, and A. Latgé

The kagome lattice, known for its geometric frustration and flat bands, provides a fertile ground for emerging correlated electronic phases. Using a self-consistent mean-field approach within the extended Hubbard model, we compute the phase diagrams of the kagome lattice as a function of on-site and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035134] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

Using a self-consistent mean-field approach within the extended Hubbard model, we compute the phase diagrams of the kagome lattice as a function of on-site and…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035134] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

item.title: Engineering Hubbard models with gated two-dimensional moiré systems
item.description:
Author(s): Yiqi Yang (杨怡奇), Yubo Yang (杨煜波), Miguel A. Morales, and Shiwei Zhang

Lattice models are powerful tools for studying strongly correlated quantum many-body systems, but their general lack of exact solutions motivates efforts to simulate them in tunable platforms. Recently, a promising new candidate has emerged for such platforms from two-dimensional materials. A subset…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035135] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

Recently, a promising new candidate has emerged for such platforms from two-dimensional materials. A subset…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035135] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Engineering Hubbard models with gated two-dimensional moiré systems

item.prism:section: Electronic structure and strongly correlated systems

item.title: Anisotropic fine structure of ion tracks in single crystals
item.description:
Author(s): Jessica Wierbik, Hendrik Heimes, Christian Notthoff, Shankar Dutt, Taleb Alwadi, Alexander Kiy, Pablo Mota-Santiago, Nigel Kirby, and Patrick Kluth

Swift heavy ion irradiation of single crystals leaves permanent damage trails, ion tracks, that are commonly assumed to be cylindrical with circular cross-sections, a view shaped by the limited resolution of conventional characterization techniques. Using an advanced small-angle x-ray scattering method here, long-standing resolution limits are overcome and ion track cross-sections are probed with Angstrom-level precision. It is found that tracks in single crystals are not circular, but instead exhibit strong, orientation-dependent anisotropy, challenging the conventional assumption and revealing how crystal structure governs track formation.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035306] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jessica Wierbik, Hendrik Heimes, Christian Notthoff, Shankar Dutt, Taleb Alwadi, Alexander Kiy, Pablo Mota-Santiago, Nigel Kirby, and Patrick Kluth

Swift heavy ion irradiation of single crystals leaves permanent damage trails, ion tracks, that are commonly assumed to be cylindrical with circular cross-sections, a view shaped by the limited resolution of conventional characterization techniques. Using an advanced small-angle x-ray scattering method here, long-standing resolution limits are overcome and ion track cross-sections are probed with Angstrom-level precision. It is found that tracks in single crystals are not circular, but instead exhibit strong, orientation-dependent anisotropy, challenging the conventional assumption and revealing how crystal structure governs track formation.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035306] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Anisotropic fine structure of ion tracks in single crystals
item.dc:creator: Jessica Wierbik, Hendrik Heimes, Christian Notthoff, Shankar Dutt, Taleb Alwadi, Alexander Kiy, Pablo Mota-Santiago, Nigel Kirby, and Patrick Kluth

item.title: Phases and criticality of the triangular lattice $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.description:
Author(s): Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song

We report the study of phases and transitions of the $\text{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model subject to a commensurate magnetic field on the triangular lattice. At filling one fermion per site, for the number of fermion flavors $2≤N≤8$, we identify three distinct phases and calculate critical intera…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045131] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song

We report the study of phases and transitions of the SU(N) Hofstadter-Hubbard model subject to a commensurate magnetic field on the triangular lattice. At filling one fermion per site, for the number of fermion flavors 2≤N≤8, we identify three distinct phases and calculate critical interaction stren…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045131] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Phases and criticality of the triangular lattice $\mathrm{SU}(N)$ Hofstadter-Hubbard model
item.dc:creator: Lu Zhang, Rongning Liu, and Xue-Yang Song

item.title: Emergent magnetism and spin liquids in an extended Hubbard model at fractional filling
item.description:
Author(s): Zhenhao Song, Urban F. P. Seifert, Leon Balents, and Hong-Chen Jiang

Motivated by twisted transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), we study an extended Hubbard model with both onsite and off-site repulsive interactions, in which Mott insulating states with concomitant charge order occur at fractional fillings. To resolve the charge ordering as well as the fate of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045132] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Zhenhao Song, Urban F. P. Seifert, Leon Balents, and Hong-Chen Jiang

Motivated by twisted transition metal dichalcogenides (TMDs), we study an extended Hubbard model with both onsite and off-site repulsive interactions, in which Mott insulating states with concomitant charge order occur at fractional fillings. To resolve the charge ordering as well as the fate of the…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045132] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

item.title: Electric and light field modulated quantum Stirling cycle in monolayer ${\mathrm{Pt}}_{2}{\mathrm{HgSe}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Xiaogang Zhang and Guojun Jin

Monolayer ${\mathrm{Pt}}_{2}{\mathrm{HgSe}}_{3}$ has been considered as the first large-gap Kane-Mele quantum spin Hall insulator and is therefore expected to exhibit distinctive topological phases and physical properties. Here, we use monolayer ${\mathrm{Pt}}_{2}{\mathrm{HgSe}}_{3}$ as the work sub…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045420] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Xiaogang Zhang and Guojun Jin

Monolayer Pt2HgSe3 has been considered as the first large-gap Kane-Mele quantum spin Hall insulator and is therefore expected to exhibit distinctive topological phases and physical properties. Here, we use monolayer Pt2HgSe3 as the work substance to demonstrate a quantum Stirling cycle driven by an …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045420] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.dc:title: Electric and light field modulated quantum Stirling cycle in monolayer ${\mathrm{Pt}}_{2}{\mathrm{HgSe}}_{3}$

item.title: Ultrafast photovoltage reversal in Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{Cd}}_{3}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ nanobelts
item.description:
Author(s): Bob Minyu Wang, Rodrigo Becerra Silva, and Dong Yu

Three-dimensional Dirac semimetal ${\mathrm{Cd}}_{3}{\mathrm{As}}_{2}$ exhibits excellent properties including high carrier mobility and a linear dispersion relation, with promising applications in ultrafast and broadband photodetection and optical communications. Here, we demonstrate that the trans…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045421] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Bob Minyu Wang, Rodrigo Becerra Silva, and Dong Yu

Three-dimensional Dirac semimetal Cd3As2 exhibits excellent properties including high carrier mobility and a linear dispersion relation, with promising applications in ultrafast and broadband photodetection and optical communications. Here, we demonstrate that the transient photovoltage in Cd3As2 na…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045421] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

item.title: Weak localization-antilocalization crossover and topological Hall response in the nearly compensated ferrimagnetic metal ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}\mathrm{NbAl}$
item.description:
Author(s): Jadupati Nag, Barnabha Bandyopadhyay, Srikanta Goswami, Anjan Bera, Y. Venkateswara, Amit Chanda, S. D. Kaushik, Hariharan Srikanth, P. D. Babu, Aftab Alam, and K. G. Suresh

Interference-induced quantum corrections to electronic transport provide a powerful route to probe localization/antilocalization phenomena in condensed-matter systems. Here, we report quantum-interference effects in a three-dimensional (3D) system, namely, the full Heusler alloy ${\mathrm{V}}_{2}\ma…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020412] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jadupati Nag, Barnabha Bandyopadhyay, Srikanta Goswami, Anjan Bera, Y. Venkateswara, Amit Chanda, S. D. Kaushik, Hariharan Srikanth, P. D. Babu, Aftab Alam, and K. G. Suresh

Interference-induced quantum corrections to electronic transport provide a powerful route to probe localization/antilocalization phenomena in condensed-matter systems. Here, we report quantum-interference effects in a three-dimensional (3D) system, namely, the full Heusler alloy V2NbAl, that are con…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020412] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

item.title: Josephson anomalous vortices
item.description:
Author(s): Dan Crawford, Stefan Ilić, Pauli Virtanen, and Tero T. Heikkilä

We show that vortices with circulating current, related with odd-frequency triplet pairing, appear in Josephson junctions where the barrier is a weak ferromagnet with strong spin-orbit coupling. By symmetry analysis we show that there is an additional term—a rotary invariant—in the superconducting f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020503] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Dan Crawford, Stefan Ilić, Pauli Virtanen, and Tero T. Heikkilä

We show that vortices with circulating current, related with odd-frequency triplet pairing, appear in Josephson junctions where the barrier is a weak ferromagnet with strong spin-orbit coupling. By symmetry analysis we show that there is an additional term—a rotary invariant—in the superconducting f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020503] Published Tue Jan 20, 2026

item.title: Measurement-only circuit of perturbed toric code on triangular lattice: Topological entanglement, 1-form symmetry, and logical qubits
item.description:
Author(s): Keisuke Kataoka, Yoshihito Kuno, Takahiro Orito, and Ikuo Ichinose

A measurement-only (quantum) circuit (MOC) gives the possibility to realize the states with rich entanglements, topological orders, and quantum memories. This work studies the MOC, in which the projective-measurement operators consist of stabilizers of the toric code and competitive local Pauli oper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024111] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Keisuke Kataoka, Yoshihito Kuno, Takahiro Orito, and Ikuo Ichinose

A measurement-only (quantum) circuit (MOC) gives the possibility to realize the states with rich entanglements, topological orders, and quantum memories. This work studies the MOC, in which the projective-measurement operators consist of stabilizers of the toric code and competitive local Pauli oper…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024111] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.title: Tuning the Chern number of a Kitaev quantum spin liquid
item.description:
Author(s): Seong Jun Kwon, Kyusung Hwang, and Suk Bum Chung

It is now well understood that non-Kitaev spin interactions can be added to the Kitaev quantum spin liquid by applying external fields. Recent years have seen intensive discussion on the possible phase transitions that these spin interactions induce. In this paper, we will show through the perturbat…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024416] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

Recent years have seen intensive discussion on the possible phase transitions that these spin interactions induce. In this paper, we will show through the perturbat…

item.title: Strain-tunable topological spin textures stabilized by Kitaev interaction in centrosymmetric vanadium halide monolayers
item.description:
Author(s): Jingman Pang, Sicheng Song, Mengyuan Lin, Meiguang Zhang, Zhe Wang, and Yun Zhang

Realizing topological spin textures such as skyrmions in two-dimensional magnets has attracted significant attention in recent years. Most efforts focus on breaking inversion symmetry to induce Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI), which is often difficult to implement experimentally. Here, using…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024417] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Jingman Pang, Sicheng Song, Mengyuan Lin, Meiguang Zhang, Zhe Wang, and Yun Zhang

Realizing topological spin textures such as skyrmions in two-dimensional magnets has attracted significant attention in recent years. Most efforts focus on breaking inversion symmetry to induce Dzyaloshinskii-Moriya interaction (DMI), which is often difficult to implement experimentally. Here, using…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 024417] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Kramers nodal line in the charge density wave state of ${\mathrm{YTe}}_{3}$ and the influence of twin domains
item.description:
Author(s): Shuvam Sarkar, Joydipto Bhattacharya, Pramod Bhakuni, Divya Jangra, Pampa Sadhukhan, Rajib Batabyal, Christos D. Malliakas, Marco Bianchi, Davide Curcio, Shubhankar Roy, Arnab Pariari, Sajal Barman, Mohammad Balal, Giovanni Di Santo, Luca Petaccia, Duck Young Chung, Yihao Wang, Vasant G. Sathe, Prabhat Mandal, Mercouri G. Kanatzidis, Philip Hofmann, Aparna Chakrabarti, and Sudipta Roy Barman

The authors demonstrate here that incommensurate charge density wave (CDW) driven inversion symmetry breaking in YTe3 produces a Kramers nodal line across the Brillouin zone. ARPES agrees well with ab initio band structure calculations when the CDW twin domains are considered. Consequently, symmetry-protected CDW shadow-band crossings with bilayer-split main bands that reach the Fermi level are identified. The noncentrosymmetric crystal structure has been established by x-ray crystallography and Raman spectroscopy. These findings establish YTe3 as a CDW-driven topological nodal-line metal.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035129] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Shuvam Sarkar, Joydipto Bhattacharya, Pramod Bhakuni, Divya Jangra, Pampa Sadhukhan, Rajib Batabyal, Christos D. Malliakas, Marco Bianchi, Davide Curcio, Shubhankar Roy, Arnab Pariari, Sajal Barman, Mohammad Balal, Giovanni Di Santo, Luca Petaccia, Duck Young Chung, Yihao Wang, Vasant G. Sathe, Prabhat Mandal, Mercouri G. Kanatzidis, Philip Hofmann, Aparna Chakrabarti, and Sudipta Roy Barman

The authors demonstrate here that incommensurate charge density wave (CDW) driven inversion symmetry breaking in YTe3 produces a Kramers nodal line across the Brillouin zone. ARPES agrees well with ab initio band structure calculations when the CDW twin domains are considered. Consequently, symmetry-protected CDW shadow-band crossings with bilayer-split main bands that reach the Fermi level are identified. The noncentrosymmetric crystal structure has been established by x-ray crystallography and Raman spectroscopy. These findings establish YTe3 as a CDW-driven topological nodal-line metal.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035129] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Kramers nodal line in the charge density wave state of ${\mathrm{YTe}}_{3}$ and the influence of twin domains

item.title: Local electronic structure and magnetic properties of double-perovskite ${\mathrm{LaCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{PrCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): Meng-Jie Huang, Peter Nagel, Andreas Eich, Michael Merz, and Stefan Schuppler

With x-ray absorption spectroscopy and x-ray magnetic circular dichroism, the element-specific electronic structure and the magnetic interactions of the double perovskite compounds ${\mathrm{PrCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{LaCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035131] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Meng-Jie Huang, Peter Nagel, Andreas Eich, Michael Merz, and Stefan Schuppler

With x-ray absorption spectroscopy and x-ray magnetic circular dichroism, the element-specific electronic structure and the magnetic interactions of the double perovskite compounds PrCo0.5Ni0.5O3 and LaCo0.5Ni0.5O3 were studied. In both compounds, Co and Ni depart from their nominal valence state of…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035131] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Local electronic structure and magnetic properties of double-perovskite ${\mathrm{LaCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$ and ${\mathrm{PrCo}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{Ni}}_{0.5}{\mathrm{O}}_{3}$

item.title: Correlated phases in rhombohedral multilayer graphene
item.description:
Author(s): Arsen Herasymchuk, Sergei G. Sharapov, Oleg V. Yazyev, and Yaroslav Zhumagulov

We investigate the emergence of correlated electron phases in rhombohedral $N$-layer graphene due to two-valley Coulomb interactions within a low-energy $k·p$ framework. Analytical expressions for Lindhard susceptibilities in intra- and intervalley channels are derived, and the critical temperatures…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035132] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Arsen Herasymchuk, Sergei G. Sharapov, Oleg V. Yazyev, and Yaroslav Zhumagulov

We investigate the emergence of correlated electron phases in rhombohedral N-layer graphene due to two-valley Coulomb interactions within a low-energy k·p framework. Analytical expressions for Lindhard susceptibilities in intra- and intervalley channels are derived, and the critical temperatures for…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035132] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.title: Anomalous thermal conductivity enhancement in twisted graphene/$h$-BN bilayers revealed by neuroevolution potential driven atomistic simulations
item.description:
Author(s): Ningxi Yang, Jincheng Yue, Xinkai Sun, Yinong Liu, Meng An, and Shiqian Hu

Twist engineering in van der Waals heterostructures has unlocked a range of novel physical phenomena, yet its influence on lattice thermal transport—particularly in heterostructures—remains underexplored. In this study, we employ a neuroevolution potential (NEP) based atomistic framework, combining …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035426] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Ningxi Yang, Jincheng Yue, Xinkai Sun, Yinong Liu, Meng An, and Shiqian Hu

Twist engineering in van der Waals heterostructures has unlocked a range of novel physical phenomena, yet its influence on lattice thermal transport—particularly in heterostructures—remains underexplored. In this study, we employ a neuroevolution potential (NEP) based atomistic framework, combining …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035426] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems
item.prism:section: Surface physics, nanoscale physics, low-dimensional systems

item.title: Inelastic electron tunneling through adatoms and molecular nanomagnets
item.description:
Author(s): Daria Kývala and Jindřich Kolorenč

The authors discuss here a theory of the inelastic electron tunneling spectra of a magnetic nanosystem (an atom or a molecule) adsorbed on a solid surface measured in a scanning tunneling microscope. They study scenarios when the tunneling electrons sequentially interact with several magnetic centers or when the magnetic centers are made out of heavy atoms with a strong spin-orbit coupling. They discuss how the exchange interaction between the nanosystem and the tunneling electrons changes when orbital moments enter the picture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035427] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Daria Kývala and Jindřich Kolorenč

The authors discuss here a theory of the inelastic electron tunneling spectra of a magnetic nanosystem (an atom or a molecule) adsorbed on a solid surface measured in a scanning tunneling microscope. They study scenarios when the tunneling electrons sequentially interact with several magnetic centers or when the magnetic centers are made out of heavy atoms with a strong spin-orbit coupling. They discuss how the exchange interaction between the nanosystem and the tunneling electrons changes when orbital moments enter the picture.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035427] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Inelastic electron tunneling through adatoms and molecular nanomagnets
item.dc:creator: Daria Kývala and Jindřich Kolorenč

item.title: Electronic structure of the kagome compound ${\mathrm{CaTi}}_{3}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}$ using high-field torque magnetometry and density functional theory
item.description:
Author(s): Kyryl Shtefiienko, Cole Phillips, Brenden R. Ortiz, David E. Graf, and Keshav Shrestha

We report systematic torque magnetometry measurements to investigate the electronic properties of the newly discovered kagome compound ${\mathrm{CaTi}}_{3}{\mathrm{Bi}}_{4}$. Electrical transport, magnetic susceptibility, and thermal measurements reveal no evidence of a magnetic ground state in this…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045128] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Kyryl Shtefiienko, Cole Phillips, Brenden R. Ortiz, David E. Graf, and Keshav Shrestha

We report systematic torque magnetometry measurements to investigate the electronic properties of the newly discovered kagome compound CaTi3Bi4. Electrical transport, magnetic susceptibility, and thermal measurements reveal no evidence of a magnetic ground state in this material. Torque data obtaine…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045128] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.title: Topological order and fractons from gauging exponential symmetries
item.description:
Author(s): Guilherme Delfino, Claudio Chamon, and Yizhi You

We broaden the scope of quantum field theory by introducing a general class of discrete gauge theories that realize either topological order or fracton behavior across dimensions. We start from translation-invariant systems endowed with unconventional charge-conservation laws, which we term exponent…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 045130] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026

item.title: Proximate spin-liquid behavior in the double trillium lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}{\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{({\mathrm{SO}}_{4})}_{3}$
item.description:
Author(s): A. Magar, K. Somesh, M. P. Saravanan, J. Sichelschmidt, Y. Skourski, M. T. F. Telling, V. A. Ginga, A. A. Tsirlin, and R. Nath

We report proximate quantum spin liquid behavior in ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}{\mathrm{Co}}_{2}$(${\mathrm{SO}}_{4}{)}_{3}$ with the magnetic ${\mathrm{Co}}^{2+}$ ions embedded on a highly frustrated three-dimensional double trillium lattice. Single-crystal and high-resolution synchrotron powder x-ray diffra…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020409] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): A. Magar, K. Somesh, M. P. Saravanan, J. Sichelschmidt, Y. Skourski, M. T. F. Telling, V. A. Ginga, A. A. Tsirlin, and R. Nath

We report proximate quantum spin liquid behavior in K2Co2(SO4)3 with the magnetic Co2+ ions embedded on a highly frustrated three-dimensional double trillium lattice. Single-crystal and high-resolution synchrotron powder x-ray diffraction experiments reveal a structural phase transition at Tt≃125K f…


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L020409] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Proximate spin-liquid behavior in the double trillium lattice antiferromagnet ${\mathrm{K}}_{2}{\mathrm{Co}}_{2}{({\mathrm{SO}}_{4})}_{3}$
item.dc:creator: A. Magar, K. Somesh, M. P. Saravanan, J. Sichelschmidt, Y. Skourski, M. T. F. Telling, V. A. Ginga, A. A. Tsirlin, and R. Nath
item.dc:rights: Personal use only, all commercial or other reuse prohibited
item.dc:subject: Magnetism
item.prism:section: Magnetism

item.title: Emergent symmetry and phase transitions on the domain wall of ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ topological orders
item.description:
Author(s): Hong-Hao Song, Chen Peng, Rui-Zhen Huang, and Long Zhang

Domain walls of topological phases can host exotic gapless states. In the one-dimensional domain wall between Z2 topological orders, the authors uncover here a hidden nonsymmorphic symmetry that enforces an emergent SU(2)1 conformal field theory. The domain wall is either gapless or symmetry breaking, reflecting its symmetry anomaly inherited from the bulk topological order. The gapless domain wall corresponds to a topological quantum critical point, fulfilling a holographic construction of topological phase transitions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041114] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Hong-Hao Song, Chen Peng, Rui-Zhen Huang, and Long Zhang

Domain walls of topological phases can host exotic gapless states. In the one-dimensional domain wall between Z2 topological orders, the authors uncover here a hidden nonsymmorphic symmetry that enforces an emergent SU(2)1 conformal field theory. The domain wall is either gapless or symmetry breaking, reflecting its symmetry anomaly inherited from the bulk topological order. The gapless domain wall corresponds to a topological quantum critical point, fulfilling a holographic construction of topological phase transitions.


[Phys. Rev. B 113, L041114] Published Fri Jan 16, 2026
item.dc:title: Emergent symmetry and phase transitions on the domain wall of ${\mathbb{Z}}_{2}$ topological orders
item.dc:creator: Hong-Hao Song, Chen Peng, Rui-Zhen Huang, and Long Zhang

A comprehensive theoretical study of MnS is conducted using linear response constrained density functional theory to determine the Hubbard $U$ parameter, structure searching to predict high-pressure phases, and first-principles calculations to investigate phase transition behavior and electronic str…

item.title: Proximity-induced spin-orbit torque in graphene on a trigonal CrSBr monolayer
item.description:
Author(s): Maedeh Rassekh and Martin Gmitra

We present a first-principles and quantum transport study of proximity-induced spin-orbit torque (SOT) in graphene on a trigonal CrSBr monolayer. Density functional theory combined with nonequilibrium Green's function calculations shows that the CrSBr substrate induces spin polarization and a sizabl…

item.title: SU(4) Kondo lattice in semiconductor moiré materials
item.description:
Author(s): Sunghoon Kim

Motivated by recent advances in transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) moiré materials, we propose TMD moiré multilayers as a platform for realizing an approximately SU(4)-symmetric triangular Kondo lattice, generalizing the concept of the double quantum dot model. Our model extends the conventional …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035127] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
item.content:encoded:
Author(s): Sunghoon Kim

Motivated by recent advances in transition metal dichalcogenide (TMD) moiré materials, we propose TMD moiré multilayers as a platform for realizing an approximately SU(4)-symmetric triangular Kondo lattice, generalizing the concept of the double quantum dot model. Our model extends the conventional …


[Phys. Rev. B 113, 035127] Published Thu Jan 15, 2026
